# Karsus, Hubris in the Blood

![](data:image/png;base64,)

  

## Legend
Binders know Karsus as a potent mortal spellcaster who attempted to steal the powers of a deity that had jurisdiction over magic. He succeeded, but realized too late that his mortal frame and soul could not contain the power. He died, and his soul remained tied to the Material Plane for ages, never becoming a petitioner. Some claim that part of it somehow still lingers there. With no planar home and no deity who would claim him, Karsus became a vestige.

## Summoning Rules
The following describes the requirements and rituals for sealing pacts with Karsus - 
- Spirit Level: 3rd
- Alignment: Neutral (N)
- Constellation: Mage
- Binding DC: 23
## Totems
You gain a +2 totem bonus on binding checks made with Karsus if one of the following requirements is met, or a +4 bonus if all requirements are met.
- The binder possesses at least 5 ranks in both Spellcraft and Knowledge Arcana.  
- The binder places a red gemstone worth at least 100 gp (ruby, garnet, red tourmaline, etc.) and places it in the center of the seal. 
- The binder places an arcane scroll of at least 3rd level in the seal that is consumed as part of the binding ceremony. 

## Ceremony
The binder places a red gemstone (100gp)  and an arcane scroll (Cl3 or lower) within the seal, then presses their bleeding hand flat against the parchment — letting their blood mingle with the ink. The scroll is consumed in the ceremony but the gemstone is not.
### Manifestation
Karsus appears silently and suddenly in the form of a great red boulder. Blood burbles up from a crack at the top of the stone and flows in a rivulet down the side facing his summoner, then pools at the base. When Karsus speaks, the blood fountains upward, its height varying based on the volume of his voice.

## Major Granted Abilities

### Karsus's Touch
You can produce a dispel magic effect with a touch. To do so, you must make a successful melee touch attack against a creature or an object as a standard action that does not provoke attacks of opportunity. You can then make a dispel check (1d20 + your effective binder level, maximum +20) against each ongoing spell currently in effect on the object or creature. The DC for this check is 11 + the spell's caster level. If you succeed on a particular check, that spell is dispelled; if you fail, it remains in effect.

If you target an object or creature that is the effect of an ongoing spell (such as a summoned monster or a conjured object), you must touch the target and make a dispel check to end the spell that brought it into being. Targeting a spell effect that is not an object or creature (such as the flames of a wall of fire) has no effect.

If the touched object is a magic item, you must make a dispel check (the DC is the item's caster level). If you succeed, all the item's magical properties are suppressed for 1d4 rounds, after which the item recovers on its own. An interdimensional interface (such as a bag of holding) is temporarily closed by this effect. A suppressed item becomes nonmagical for the duration, but its physical properties are unchanged, so a suppressed magic sword is still a masterwork sword. After using this ability, it becomes expended for 5 rounds.

**Capstone Empowerment:** The effects of Karsus’s Touch are enhanced as if the binder possesses the Dispel Focus Feat (+2 bonus on the check).  This ability is only applied to Karsus’s Touch and not to any other casting of Dispel Magic if the binder is able to do so.

## Minor Granted Abilities

### Karsus's Senses 
You can sense magic auras as easily as others can detect odors, and concentrating on them reveals them to your sight. When you concentrate (a standard action), you automatically detect the number of different magical auras within 30 feet, and the power of the most potent aura within 30 feet, as though you had cast detect magic on the area and concentrated for 2 rounds. You do not need to concentrate to maintain this effect, but concentrating for 1 additional round reveals the location and strength of the auras in the area. A third round of concentration allows you to determine the school of a particular aura automatically. Additional rounds of concentration reveal the schools of other auras.

You cannot use this ability if you are blinded, but otherwise it functions as detect magic for the purposes of concealment and the materials pierced. If you encounter an aura of overwhelming strength, however, you are dazzled so long as it remains within range of Karsus's senses.

### Karsus's Will
You can use spell trigger items, such as wands and staves, as if you were a wizard of your effective binder level.

### Heavy Magic
As a free action, you may increase the save DC for the effects of any magic item you use by 2. This does not affect consumable items. The binder can use this ability 3+ their Charisma modifier times a day.

### Scent of the Arcane
The binder receives an insight bonus of ½ their binder level to Spellcraft and Knowledge Arcana skills. 

## Signs and Influence
Karsus affects you in the following ways:
**Physical Sign:** You bleed more than normal from wounds. Even a small scratch releases a sanguine flood. This effect does not deal extra damage.
**Personality Influence:** You take on some of the arrogance for which Karsus was famous in his mortal life. He requires that you make Bluff or Intimidate checks rather than Diplomacy checks to influence others.
**Favored Ally:** Arcane Spellcasters, those who worship no deities
**Favored Enemy:** Divine Spellcasters, Outsiders (divine servants)
**Vestigial Bond (Boon):** The binder may select one arcane school or focused arcane school as listed here - [https://www.aonprd.com/WizardSchools.aspx](https://www.aonprd.com/WizardSchools.aspx); They gain access to 1 (one) of the first-level abilities for the duration of their pact, using their Charisma modifier in place of Intelligence modifier where applicable.  This replaces Heavy Magic.
